title: thalictrum majus subsp. olympicum
intro: Thalictrum majus subsp. olympicum, also known as the Olympic meadow rue, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This perennial plant is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Thalictrum majus subsp. olympicum are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
